hero.title: API Tester
hero.description: Save and run lightweight API requests from the browser without sending URLs, headers, tokens, or payloads through Ansiversa servers.

flow.title: Create -> Run -> Review
steps: Create a local request with method, URL, headers, and body.
Run the request explicitly from the browser when CORS allows.
Review local response status, timing, preview, and run history.

differences.title: A private V1 for quick API checks
differences.description: API Tester keeps sensitive testing details in the user's browser while still supporting practical request and response review.

items.title: Saved requests
items.description: Store reusable request metadata locally for quick reruns.

items.title: Explicit execution
items.description: Browser fetch only runs after the user chooses to run a saved request.

items.title: CORS-aware feedback
items.description: When a target API blocks browser calls, the app explains the limitation instead of routing data through a backend proxy.

feel.title: Fast enough for everyday endpoint checks
feel.description: The workflow keeps request setup, local collections, run history, and stats close together.

items.title: Create in a drawer
items.description: Add or edit request details without leaving the current workflow.

items.title: Rerun from collections
items.description: Search saved requests and launch a browser run with one explicit action.

items.title: Review locally
items.description: Keep status, duration, and response previews in browser storage only.

audiences.title: Who uses API Tester
audiences.description: Useful for builders who need quick browser-based endpoint checks without introducing backend storage.

items.title: Developers
items.description: Save common requests and rerun endpoints during implementation.

items.title: Technical teams
items.description: Validate browser-accessible APIs while keeping sensitive request data local.

items.title: Learners
items.description: Practice request and response concepts in a simple local workflow.

resourceSection.title: API testing essentials
resourceSection.description: A compact local workflow for request setup, execution, and review.

items.title: Request
items.description: Method, URL, headers, and body saved in browser storage.

items.title: Run
items.description: Explicit browser fetch execution when the target API allows cross-origin calls.

items.title: History
items.description: Status, timing, and previews stored locally for later review.

groupedSection.title: Build a local API testing routine
groupedSection.description: Move from setup to collections to history without leaving the mini app.

items.name: Workspace
items.description: Create requests through the shared form drawer.

items.name: Collections
items.description: Search, edit, rerun, delete, or clear saved requests.

items.name: History
items.description: Review local runs, status codes, durations, and error notes.

items.name: Insights
items.description: Track saved requests, run totals, success/error counts, average time, and most-used method.

technicalSection.title: Browser-local by design
technicalSection.description: API Tester follows the shared Ansiversa mini-app architecture while avoiding backend request execution in V1.
items: Lazy-loaded frontend module with shared overview, page header, form drawer, empty state, feedback, and stats components.
Versioned localStorage keys: ansiversa.api-tester.requests.v1 and ansiversa.api-tester.runs.v1.
No backend proxy, cloud sync, API token storage service, heavy editor library, or server-side execution runtime.

finalCta.title: Explore browser-local API testing
finalCta.description: Create a saved request, run it explicitly from the browser, and review the local result.
